issues.blurb: Chains, cassettes, and chainrings stretch and wear with mileage, and skipping or poor shifting follows if the chain is not replaced in time.
Rim and disc brake pads wear down with use and weather, reducing stopping power and risking damage to the rim or rotor if run too far.
Tubeless sealant dries out and needs topping up, and thin road tires still pick up punctures and pinch flats that need attention.
Headset and bottom bracket bearings develop play, grinding, or creaking from rain and road grit, especially on higher-mileage bikes.
Shift cables stretch and housings contaminate, or electronic and hydraulic systems drift, causing slow or imprecise gear changes.
Spoke tension shifts over time and wheels go out of true, causing rub and wobble until re-tensioned.

narrative.headline: What road bike owners report most
narrative.summary: These are issues commonly reported by road cyclists as a general reference, not expert-verified data and possibly outdated. Drivetrain wear, brake pads, and tubeless or flat tire issues tend to come up most.

issues.title: Drivetrain Wear
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~26%) — chains, cassettes, and rings wear with mileage and cause skipping if not replaced.

issues.title: Brake Pads (Rim / Disc)
issues.blurb: Commonly reported (~22%) — pads wear with use and weather, cutting stopping power and risking rim or rotor damage.

issues.title: Tubeless Sealant & Flats
issues.blurb: Often reported (~20%) — sealant dries out and thin tires still pick up punctures and pinch flats.

issues.title: Headset & Bottom Bracket Bearings
issues.blurb: Reported (~16%) — bearings develop play, grinding, or creaking from rain and grit.

issues.title: Shifting (Cable or Hydraulic)
issues.blurb: Reported (~12%) — cables stretch or systems drift, causing slow or imprecise shifts.

issues.title: Wheel Truing
issues.blurb: Reported (~4%) — spoke tension shifts and wheels go out of true, causing rub and wobble.
